implementation: Use Context or state management for deeply shared state
example: createContext useContext for theme auth user

implementation: Use useMemo for expensive computations useCallback for stable callbacks
Avoid creating objects/arrays in render use refs or state
Extract reusable logic into custom hooks
Use useState for simple local component state
Use useReducer for complex state with multiple sub-values
Always return cleanup function from useEffect when needed
Include all dependencies in useEffect dependency array
Use stable unique keys for lists never use index if list changes
Use controlled inputs with value and onChange
Define event handlers with useCallback if passed to children
Keep components small and focused single responsibility
example: Split into Container and Presentation components

implementation: Use Suspense for loading states with lazy components
Use Error Boundaries for graceful error handling
example: class ErrorBoundary extends Component with getDerivedStateFromError

implementation: Use useRef for DOM access and mutable values
Use createPortal for modals tooltips that need to escape parent
Split Context by update frequency to prevent rerenders
Define PropTypes or TypeScript interfaces for components
example: interface Props { title: string; onClick: () => void }

implementation: Prefer composition over props for flexible components
Write components that are easy to test
example: Export pure functions separately add data-testid

implementation: Use semantic HTML elements in JSX
Use React synthetic events they're normalized
Use short-circuit for conditional rendering
Use Fragments to avoid unnecessary div wrappers
Use CSS Modules or styled-components for scoped styles
